goal: Refactor the decision handoff so a redirect becomes a host-composed next instruction the human approves before launch — replace direct-enqueue with a resolved -> composing -> composed -> approved status machine (server + client only).

slides.title: Why this sprint: the handoff was a black box
content: Before: POST /api/minutes recorded the decision AND immediately enqueued the next sprint with goal=directive
The human's raw redirect went straight to the agents — no visible 'here is what I will work on next'
Piece 2 of the reframe: redirect -> host composes a crisp instruction -> human approves -> only then launch
Scope this sprint: server + client only; scripts/poll.mjs (the daemon compose loop) untouched
slides.narration: Quick framing before the details. Until this sprint, when you resolved a decision slide, the server did two things at once: it recorded your decision and it immediately queued the next sprint using whatever you typed as the goal. That meant your raw redirect went straight to the agents with nothing shown back to you. The standing direction from our last meeting was to make this a real handoff: you redirect, the host agent composes a crisp next instruction plus minutes, that instruction is shown back to you, and only after you approve does it launch. This sprint builds the server and client half of exactly that. We did not touch the poller daemon — that is the director's.

slides.title: DB layer: a status machine on the minutes row
content: Idempotent migration: CREATE TABLE has new cols; 5x ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS for the existing deployed DB
Legacy payload column left as-is, never referenced — new INSERTs omit it
insertMinutes now writes directive/answers columns, status='resolved' (no payload)
claimPendingCompose: FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED claim of oldest 'resolved' -> 'composing'
setComposed / getMinutesForBriefing (camelCase) / approveMinutes (atomic txn, enqueues + marks 'approved')
approveMinutes throws 'not found' (404) vs 'not composed' (409) so routes can discriminate
Spec match: all 10 acceptance criteria met; 12 new db tests pass
slides.narration: First lane: the database. The minutes table is now the spine of a four-state machine — resolved, composing, composed, approved. The tricky part was that the deployed database already has a minutes table with a NOT NULL payload column, so the migration had to be idempotent for both a fresh database and the existing one. We did that by declaring the new columns in CREATE TABLE for fresh databases and adding five ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS statements for the existing one. The old payload column is left untouched and never referenced again. insertMinutes now writes dedicated directive and answers columns and stamps status resolved. We added claimPendingCompose, which atomically claims the oldest resolved row using FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED — the same pattern as the sprint queue — plus setComposed, getMinutesForBriefing, and approveMinutes, which enqueues the sprint and flips the row to approved in a single transaction. Crucially, approveMinutes throws distinct errors for an unknown id versus a row that is not yet composed, so the routes can return a clean 404 versus 409. Implementation matched the spec exactly: all ten acceptance criteria met, twelve new database tests green.

slides.title: Server routes: record decoupled from enqueue
content: POST /api/minutes — no token, records 'resolved' only, returns { minutesId }; no enqueue
GET /api/compose/pending — token-gated, daemon claims oldest 'resolved', 204 when none
POST /api/minutes/:id/instruction — token-gated, host posts { goal, minutesText } -> 'composed'
GET /api/minutes?briefingId=N — no token, browser polls; 400 on missing/non-integer id
POST /api/minutes/:id/approve — no token, enqueues (goal else composed_goal), 409/404 on bad state
renderMinutes helper and enqueueSprint import fully removed — no dead path, no compat layer
Spec match: 8 acceptance criteria met; 24 route tests pass, GET /api/next-sprint tests preserved
slides.narration: Second lane: the routes. The old single-step POST /api/minutes is gone, replaced by five endpoints that form the handoff bus. POST /api/minutes now only records a resolved decision and returns the minutes id — it no longer enqueues anything. The daemon claims work through GET /api/compose/pending, which is token-gated and returns 204 when nothing is pending. The host agent posts the composed result to POST /api/minutes/:id/instruction, also token-gated. The browser polls GET /api/minutes by briefing id with no token, and finally posts the human's approval to POST /api/minutes/:id/approve, which is where the enqueue now happens — using the edited goal if provided, otherwise the composed goal, with a 409 if the row was not composed yet and a 404 for an unknown id. Per the master rules, we removed the old renderMinutes helper and the enqueueSprint import entirely — no compatibility layer, no dead path. Twenty-four route tests pass and the next-sprint poller tests are preserved unchanged.

slides.title: Client: the two-step compose-then-approve flow
content: Step 1: Approve/Redirect -> POST /api/minutes -> 'Composing the next instruction…' banner (data-testid=composing)
Polls GET /api/minutes?briefingId=N every 2000ms until row status='composed'; transient errors ignored
Step 2: editable textarea 'Next sprint goal' seeded from composedGoal + 'Approve & launch' button
Approve -> POST /api/minutes/:id/approve { goal } -> 'Next sprint queued' (minutes-confirmed)
Error/retry on both POSTs; interval cleared on unmount (no state update after unmount)
SlideStage.jsx unchanged — DecisionControls still calls onDecide(outcome, direction)
Spec match: 8 'minutes submission' tests deleted, 10 two-step tests added; 63/63 client tests green
slides.narration: Third lane: the client. The decision slide is now genuinely two-step, and this visible composed-instruction-then-approve moment is the whole point. When you click Approve or Redirect, the browser posts your direction and immediately shows a 'composing the next instruction' banner while it polls every two seconds. Once the host agent's composed instruction lands — the row flips to composed — the browser drops you into an editable textarea titled 'Next sprint goal', pre-filled with what the agent proposes. You can edit it freely. Clicking 'Approve and launch' posts your possibly-edited goal and shows 'Next sprint queued'. Both posts have error and retry paths, and the polling interval is cleaned up on unmount so there are no stray state updates. We did not touch SlideStage — the decision controls still call onDecide the same way. The old eight-test minutes-submission block was deleted and replaced with ten tests covering the full two-step flow with mocked fetch and fake timers. All sixty-three client tests are green.

slides.title: Docs updated to current state
content: docs/README.md: POST /api/minutes section rewritten as the resolved->composing->composed->approved status machine
All five routes documented with token requirements and status codes (201/204/400/404/409)
db.js section lists every function signature incl. claimPendingCompose/setComposed/getMinutesForBriefing/approveMinutes
minutes table columns documented; enqueueSprint noted as internal-only (called by approveMinutes)
MeetingView client section describes the two-step state machine and 2000ms polling
Integration resolved one docs conflict: kept routes-branch (status-machine) wording over stale db-branch text
slides.narration: Docs were kept current the whole way through. The README's minutes section was rewritten from the old 'records and enqueues' description into the full status machine, with every one of the five routes documented including which require the bearer token and their exact status codes. The database section now lists every function signature, the full minutes table shape, and notes that enqueueSprint is now internal-only — called by approveMinutes, never by a route. The client section describes the two-step state machine and the two-second polling. At integration the only conflict was in the docs, where two branches had rewritten the same section; we kept the routes-branch wording since the database-branch text was stale, and confirmed no contradictions remain.

slides.title: Decision: server+client half landed green — what next?
content: Done: status machine (db + routes), two-step client approve-before-launch, docs; 63/63 server + 63/63 client tests green
Merged to main on first pass, no test-loop iterations; scripts/poll.mjs untouched as required
Open question this leaves: the host-compose agent (the daemon loop calling /api/compose/pending + /api/minutes/:id/instruction) is the director's piece — not built here
Approve: proceed to wire/verify the daemon compose loop end-to-end against these endpoints
Redirect: tell me a different next target (e.g. minutes display UI, compose-prompt design, error UX hardening)
slides.narration: So where we landed: the server and client half of the compose-then-approve handoff is fully built, reviewed, and merged to main on the first integration pass with no test fixups. Both suites are fully green, sixty-three tests each, and we left the poller daemon alone as instructed. The natural next step is the other half — the host-compose agent that the daemon runs: it polls compose-pending, generates the crisp instruction, and posts it to the instruction endpoint. That is your piece as director, so I want your call. If you approve, the next sprint wires and verifies that daemon compose loop end-to-end against these new endpoints. If you would rather redirect — say, build the minutes-history display, harden the error UX, or design the compose prompt — tell me the target and I will compose the next instruction for your approval.

artifacts.specDeltas:
Three non-overlapping specs, all implemented as specified with no spec-wrong escalations.

[minutes-status-machine-db] server/src/db.js. Delta: insertMinutes signature changed from ({briefingId,outcome,payload}) to ({briefingId,outcome,directive,answers}); writes dedicated columns + status='resolved', no payload write. Added claimPendingCompose() (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED claim of oldest 'resolved' -> 'composing'), setComposed({id,composedGoal,composedMinutes}) -> 'composed' (true/false on rowCount), getMinutesForBriefing(briefingId) -> camelCase array oldest-first, approveMinutes({id,goal}) atomic transaction enqueuing sprint_queue and setting 'approved', throwing 'not found' (unknown id) vs 'not composed' (wrong status). Schema idempotent: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS with new columns + 5 ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS; legacy payload column left untouched. All 10 acceptance criteria met.

[minutes-status-machine-routes] server/src/app.js + server/test/minutes.test.js. Delta: POST /api/minutes no longer enqueues (returns {minutesId} only, 201). Added GET /api/compose/pending (token, 204/200), POST /api/minutes/:id/instruction (token, 200/404/400), GET /api/minutes?briefingId=N (no token, 400 on missing/non-integer), POST /api/minutes/:id/approve (no token, 201/409/404 via error-message discrimination). renderMinutes helper removed; enqueueSprint import removed. GET /api/next-sprint and Q&A/briefings routes intact. All 8 acceptance criteria met.

[decision-slide-two-step] client/src/MeetingView.jsx + tests. Delta: submitStatus enum expanded from (null|pending|confirmed|error) to (null|pending|composing|composed|approving|approved|submit-error|approve-error). Step 1 effect POSTs /api/minutes once (guarded by submitted ref), reads minutesId, enters 'composing'. Second effect polls GET /api/minutes?briefingId every 2000ms until status='composed', seeds approveGoal from composedGoal. Step 2 renders editable textarea (aria-label='Next sprint goal') + 'Approve & launch' -> POST /api/minutes/:id/approve {goal} -> 'Next sprint queued'. SlideStage.jsx unchanged. 8 old 'minutes submission' tests deleted, 10 new two-step tests added. All acceptance criteria met (one cosmetic note: spec said 9 pre-existing MeetingView tests, 10 actually existed and all preserved).

artifacts.testOutput:
Integration test run (testStatus: green, no build/test-loop iterations consumed):
- npm run build: succeeded (vite build, 32 modules, dist emitted)
- server: node --test -> tests 63, pass 63, fail 0 (includes minutes.test.js 24 route tests, minutes.db.test.js 12 db tests, qa.routes.test.js, qa.db.test.js, version.test.js)
- client: vitest run -> 5 files, 63 passed (10 MeetingView briefing-loading tests preserved, 10 new two-step decision-handoff tests, 12 SlideStage tests unchanged, 17 useMeetingState, 7 Router, 7 QAPanel)
server/src/db.js merged byte-identically from both server branches (no conflict). Only docs/README.md conflicted and was resolved keeping the routes-branch status-machine wording.
artifacts.docsChanged:
docs/README.md updated to current state (no history notes):
- POST /api/minutes section fully rewritten from the old 'records + enqueues, returns {minutesId, queuedSprintId}' into the resolved->composing->composed->approved status machine.
- All five routes documented with token requirements and status codes: POST /api/minutes (no token, 201 {minutesId}, 400); GET /api/compose/pending (token, 200/204); POST /api/minutes/:id/instruction (token, 200/404/400); GET /api/minutes?briefingId=N (no token, 400 on missing/non-integer); POST /api/minutes/:id/approve (no token, 201/409/404).
- db.js section lists every function signature including the four new status-machine functions; notes schema idempotency (CREATE TABLE + ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS); documents the full minutes table columns; notes enqueueSprint is now internal-only (called by approveMinutes, not by routes).
- MeetingView client section describes the eight-state submitStatus machine, the StrictMode-guarded single POST, the 2000ms polling, the editable compose-review textarea, and the approve-then-queued flow.
Integration ran a docs consistency pass confirming no contradictions between the server status-machine description and the client two-step description.
artifacts.reviewerVerdicts:
All three lanes: review pass=true, specWrong=false.

[minutes-status-machine-db] PASS. All 12 new minutes.db.test.js tests pass; all 49 server tests green at lane time. Schema idempotency verified (CREATE TABLE has all new columns + no payload; 5 ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS). insertMinutes/claimPendingCompose/setComposed/getMinutesForBriefing/approveMinutes match specified signatures and semantics; error discrimination (not found vs not composed) exact; atomic transaction + FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED correct. Docs current.

[minutes-status-machine-routes] PASS (after attempt-1 blocker fixed). Attempt 1 failed only because the worktree's db.js was the pre-change version, so app.js ESM imports of new exports were unresolved and every test file died at module load. Fix: synced db.js to the byte-identical db-lane version (conflict-free at integrate). All 24 minutes route tests + 19 other server tests green. AC verified: POST /api/minutes returns 201 {minutesId} with enqueueSprint absent (grep confirmed); all five routes correct status codes/token gating; renderMinutes + enqueueSprint import removed (grep confirmed); next-sprint tests preserved.

[decision-slide-two-step] PASS. All 63 client tests green. Verified: single guarded POST /api/minutes; composing state hides slide stage; 2000ms poll resolves to compose-review with pre-filled aria-label='Next sprint goal' textarea; edited vs unedited approve goal; minutes-confirmed 'Next sprint queued'; submit-error/approve-error + retry paths; unmount clears interval. SlideStage.jsx untouched (12 tests pass). Only discrepancy cosmetic: spec said 9 pre-existing MeetingView tests, 10 existed and all preserved — not a defect.
